ph.note: Below target range (6.5–7.5) but above danger threshold. Temperature probe was dislodged May 4th; pH readings during that period are unreliable. Current pH stable since probe was restored.

reasoning: pH has drifted into the 5.6–5.7 range over the past 24 hours (24hr_change: −0.14), but this is still above the hard danger line of 6.0. The 20% water change on May 5th would normally raise pH; the current reading suggests either the tank's carbonate buffering is genuinely weak or the probe displacement on May 4th corrupted baseline data. A cautious re-baseline water test would clarify the true trend before the cycle completes.

ph.note: Manual test (pH 6.6) diverges from probe (5.61); probe likely requires recalibration despite Apr 26 calibration claim

reasoning: Temperature stable and excellent; TDS in range. pH probe reading (5.61) conflicts with today's manual test (6.6), suggesting sensor drift—likely culprit given recalibration occurred 10 days ago and temperature probe displacement on May 4 may have compromised nearby sensor calibration. Manual test value (6.6) should be treated as ground truth pending probe service.

ph.note: Probe reading diverges from manual test (6.6). Probe may require recalibration despite recent service.

reasoning: pH probe is reading 5.59 while Toby's manual test shows 6.6 — a significant 1.0-point gap that suggests the probe needs recalibration despite being freshly calibrated on April 26th. Temperature and TDS are stable and healthy; the colony has been thriving for 23 days post-introduction.

actions.note: Run another manual pH test to confirm whether probe is drifting or if the earlier 6.6 test was anomalous. One data point is not enough to trigger recalibration.
